title: Fix 'Unauthorized: Only users can access settings' error on Settings page

requirements.text: Fix the Settings page so that it does not attempt to load settings when the user is not authenticated. If the user is not logged in, show a prompt to log in rather than calling the backend settings endpoint, which currently throws 'Unauthorized: Only users can access settings'.

quotes: Failed to load settings. Please try again later.
Unauthorized: Only users can access settings

acceptanceCriteria: When a user is not authenticated, the Settings page does not call the backend settings endpoint.
When a user is not authenticated, the Settings page displays a message prompting the user to log in rather than an error.
When a user is authenticated, the Settings page loads settings successfully without any 'Unauthorized' error.
No 'Failed to load settings' error message appears for authenticated users.

requirements.text: Fix the useAccountSettings hook to guard all backend calls with an authentication check. Only invoke backend settings queries and mutations when a valid session/actor is available; return appropriate idle/unauthenticated state otherwise.

quotes: Unauthorized: Only users can access settings

acceptanceCriteria: The useAccountSettings hook does not issue a backend request when no authenticated session is present.
The hook returns a clear unauthenticated state (e.g., disabled query, null data) when the user is not logged in.
The hook fetches and mutates settings correctly when the user is authenticated.

constraints: Do not modify files listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
All Motoko logic must remain in the single main actor file.
nonGoals: Adding new settings fields or changing the settings UI layout.
Changing the backend authorization logic or removing authentication requirements from the backend.
